reason: Both answers reach the same correct verdict and cover the key points equally well: SKIP LOCKED handling the throughput trivially, transactional enqueue as the underrated advantage, concrete tool names, and sensible switch triggers to a dedicated broker. Neither has factual errors. Extra details in each (A's tool list breadth and I/O caveat vs B's peak-rate estimate and migration-ease note) are minor elaborations, not decision-changing additions. Overall quality is comparable.
